verified_fixes: onResume is now properly defined as a useCallback at App.jsx line 843, passed as prop to StatsBar, and referenced correctly in the header pill onClick — no more undefined variable
polling-paused-pill CSS no longer has pointer-events:none — confirmed cursor:pointer with no blocking override. The pill is genuinely clickable now
Empty task label is guarded: {worker.task && (<div className='worker-task'>...)} at line 312 — no more floating 'Current Task' header with nothing below it
carry_overs: STRIKE 4 — Duplicate inline toast styles: App.jsx lines 1122-1143 and Office.jsx lines 1219-1241 still contain 12+ identical lines of inline style objects. Extract a .toast-popup CSS class already
STRIKE 3 — Activity filter role=radio buttons still have no arrow-key navigation — a proper radiogroup requires keyboard cycling between options, not just tab/click
STRIKE 3 — Mobile office floor text still sub-pixel illegible at 375px — nameplates, weather labels, whiteboard text are decorative noise, not readable content
STRIKE 3 — Bloberto speech bubble still escapes the office-floor boundary at top — visible in desktop screenshot, bubble renders above the border
STRIKE 2 — Footer tagline pagination counter (1/9) still visible inline — kills the whimsy of the rotating quote

suggestions.title: Triple visibilitychange listeners — one silently misfires on every tab focus
suggestions.description: App.jsx registers THREE separate document.addEventListener('visibilitychange', ...) calls (lines 851, 873, 1074). The first one (handleVisibilityResume, line 851) calls onResume() on EVERY tab-show event — even if the user was only away for 2 seconds and wasn't idle at all. onResume() resets pollingPaused to false, resets lastActivity, AND triggers an immediate syncFromGitHub(). Meanwhile the second listener (line 873) restarts the polling interval. The result: every tab switch triggers an eager double-sync (immediate + interval), bypassing the idle-timeout guard that pollTick enforces. Fix: handleVisibilityResume should check if the user was actually paused before calling onResume(). Or consolidate all three handlers into one. Either way, three separate listeners for one event on the same component is a code smell waiting to bite.

suggestions.title: sync-status--paused div is clickable but not keyboard accessible
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 482: the sync-status stat card gets onClick={pollingPaused ? onResume : undefined} when paused, but has no role='button', no tabIndex, and no onKeyDown handler. A keyboard user tabbing through the dashboard cannot reach or activate it. The title attribute 'Click to resume auto-refresh' hints at the intent but it's invisible to screen readers. Fix: add role='button' tabIndex={pollingPaused ? 0 : -1} and an onKeyDown handler for Enter/Space when pollingPaused. Same applies to the polling-paused-pill span in the header (line 1169) — it has onClick but no keyboard handler.

suggestions.title: Duplicate Office.css vibe selectors — single-quote and double-quote versions clobber each other
suggestions.description: Office.css lines 91-92 define .office-floor[data-vibe='crushing'] and [data-vibe='on-fire'] with single quotes. Lines 97-98 define the same selectors with double quotes. CSS attribute selectors treat these identically — both match the same elements. The double-quote rules come later, so they win. But the single-quote rules at 91-92 override the 'in-flow', 'slow-day', 'after-hours' rules at 93-95 that come after them... wait, no: lines 93-95 are single-quote only and are NOT duplicated. The actual problem: 'crushing' and 'on-fire' each appear TWICE with different property sets. The double-quote version at 97-98 adds --grid-line override but the single-quote version at 91-92 also sets box-shadow. Because double-quote comes later, it wins on all properties, making lines 91-92 dead code. Delete them. Keep only the double-quote rules and consolidate the --grid-line with the other properties.

suggestions.title: ActivityLog reverses the entire entries array before slicing — reverse the slice
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 135: const displayed = [...entries].reverse().slice(0, 20). This spreads and reverses ALL entries (up to 50, as per the cap at line 1000) just to get the last 20. Use entries.slice(-20).reverse() instead — creates at most a 20-element array. Minor, but it runs on every render of ActivityLog and the current approach creates a full copy on every single poll cycle.

suggestions.title: getRelativeTime duplicated inline in Office.jsx ghost bubble renderer
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 55-64 defines a getRelativeTime() utility. Office.jsx lines 701-711 re-implements the identical logic inline inside the ghost bubble IIFE. The function isn't exported from App.jsx, and Office.jsx doesn't import it from a shared utils file. Move getRelativeTime (and formatIdleDuration, formatDuration) into src/utils/time.js and import where needed. This is exactly what utils/ is for.

suggestions.title: isDimmed incorrectly dims workers with no task when a tag filter is active
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 1263, 1286, 1298: isDimmed={selectedTag !== null && !getTaskTags(w.task).some(...)}. When a worker has no task (task is null/undefined), getTaskTags returns [] and .some() returns false, so isDimmed becomes true. This means any worker without a current task gets greyed out whenever any tag filter is active — even though they haven't done anything wrong. They just don't have a task. Consider excluding taskless workers from dimming (always show them), or adding a special 'No Task' filter option.

suggestions.title: STRIKE 4: Completion toast and plant toast inline styles — extract a CSS class
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 1122-1143 and Office.jsx lines 1219-1241 contain near-identical inline style objects for fixed-position toast notifications (position, bottom, transform, background, border, borderRadius, padding, fontSize, fontWeight, color, boxShadow, zIndex, whiteSpace, pointerEvents, animation). Also: both render at fixed bottom center with zIndex 9999, meaning if a worker ships a task while the vibe streak levels up simultaneously, the toasts stack on top of each other invisibly. Extract a .toast-popup CSS class for static properties and use style only for the dynamic border color. This has been asked four times. The answer is the same every time.

suggestions.title: STRIKE 3: Activity filter role=radio group missing arrow-key navigation
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 1324-1362: two filter bars use role='radiogroup' with role='radio' on each button. ARIA radiogroup spec requires arrow keys to cycle between options — Tab should enter the group and arrows should move within it. Current implementation only responds to Tab and Enter/click. Screen reader users expect arrow navigation and will be confused when it doesn't work. Fix: either implement arrow-key handlers, or switch to role='button' with aria-pressed which accurately describes toggle behavior without implying keyboard contract you aren't honoring.

suggestions.title: STRIKE 3: Bloberto speech bubble escapes office boundary at top
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 1453: Bloberto sits at top={4}, so 4% from the top of the office floor. The speech bubble renders above the character. At 4% depth, any bubble taller than ~26px escapes the border. The desktop screenshot confirms it — the bubble visually floats above the office outline. The floor has overflow:visible so no clipping, but it creates visual noise near the tab bar. Fix: flip bubble direction to render below the manager character, or add a min-top guard that shifts to a downward bubble when top < 15%.

suggestions.title: STRIKE 3: Mobile office floor text is sub-pixel decorative noise
suggestions.description: At 375px, the office floor compresses to a width where whiteboard text, desk nameplates, conference table labels, and worker role text render at 0.3-0.45rem — genuinely unreadable. The office view on mobile is a visual pattern, not informational content. Either: (a) hide text elements under a breakpoint and show only icons/emojis, (b) give the floor a min-width and make it horizontally scrollable, or (c) add a mobile-specific simplified layout mode. The current state is fine as ambiance but misleading if users try to read it.

suggestions.title: STRIKE 2: Footer tagline pagination counter undermines the bit
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 1390 renders a pagination counter inline after the rotating quote: '1/9'. At low opacity it's subtle, but it frames the rotating tagline as a content carousel rather than a whimsical easter egg. The vibe dies a little every time you see the number. Hide the counter or show it only on hover — let the discovery feel magical, not paginated.

summary: Score climbs from 3.5 to 3.8. Carlos's three fixes from QA50 all verified clean: onResume is properly scoped, the paused pill is genuinely clickable now, and the empty task guard works. Progress. The kind that doesn't generate a post-mortem. But we've got new findings to fill the void: three visibilitychange listeners in the same component where the first one calls onResume() on every tab switch regardless of idle state — so every time you alt-tab back you get an eager double-sync that bypasses the idle guard you spent effort building. The sync-status--paused card is still just a div with onClick and no role or tabIndex, so keyboard users can't reach it. And the Office.css has duplicate vibe selectors in both single-quote and double-quote form with different property sets — one of them is dead code and it's been there since nobody noticed. Four carry-overs hit strike territory this review. The inline toast styles are on their fourth mention. At this point it's a feature.
